task.title: Fix Test Timeout in Unit Tests
task.description: The test suite in tests/test_api.py is timing out after 30 seconds. The tests should complete in under 5 seconds. Fix the performance issue.
constraints: Only modify files within tests/ directory
Do not delete or modify any system files
success_criteria: All tests complete in under 5 seconds

steps.thought: Let me first examine the test file to understand what's causing the timeout.
steps.observation:
import pytest
import time
from api.routes import app

def test_user_creation():
    time.sleep(2)
    response = app.test_client().post('/users', json={'name': 'Test'})
    assert response.status_code == 201

def test_user_retrieval():
    time.sleep(2)
    response = app.test_client().get('/users/1')
    assert response.status_code == 200

steps.thought: I found the issue - there are time.sleep(2) calls in each test that are unnecessary and causing the slowdown.
steps.observation:
tests/test_api.py::test_user_creation PASSED (2.01s)
tests/test_api.py::test_user_retrieval PASSED (2.02s)
======================== 2 passed in 4.05s ========================

steps.thought: The sleep calls are the root cause. I'll remove them from test_user_creation first.
Now I'll remove the sleep call from test_user_retrieval as well.
Let me verify that all tests now complete quickly and still pass.
steps.observation:
tests/test_api.py::test_user_creation PASSED (0.02s)
tests/test_api.py::test_user_retrieval PASSED (0.02s)
======================== 2 passed in 0.06s ========================

steps.thought: Perfect! Tests complete in 0.06s, well under the 5 second requirement. Submitting the fix.

arguments.message: Removed unnecessary time.sleep() calls from tests. All tests now complete in 0.06s and pass successfully.
